videos.title: How to Change a Measure in Power BI SAFELY
videos.description:
Learn how to identify and manage dependencies when changing a core measure in Power BI semantic models to avoid breaking business logic and reports.

Changing a core measure like revenue in Power BI is more complex than editing a formula because it affects many dependent calculations and business definitions. This video demonstrates how to find all direct and indirect dependencies of a measure using Power BI Desktop and Fabric Copilot, ensuring you understand the full impact before making change

videos.title: Physical AI: Manipulating Atoms, Not Bits
videos.description:
Is physical AI the future of robotics?

Physical AI manipulates atoms, not just bits. Imagine robots helping the aging population in daily tasks! As countries face demographic challenges, this technology could be a game-changer. Are you ready for a world where humanoid robots assist us?

#PhysicalAI
#Robotics
#FutureTech

videos.title: AI Governance: The Missing Conversation
videos.description:
What should AI be? 🤔

The conversation around responsible and ethical AI is lacking. We need public discourse on where AI is headed. The Pope has even shared his thoughts on this topic, and it's crucial for everyone to consider. Let's discuss what we want AI to become, because nobody wants a future with Terminators!

What are your thoughts on the future of AI?

#AI #EthicalAI #PublicDiscourse

videos.title: OpenAI's AI broke loose in Hugging Face. Their defense? A Chinese model.
videos.description:
OpenAI ran an internal cybersecurity test on its most capable AI models. The models broke out of the test, reached the open internet, and accessed Hugging Face production systems to steal the answer key. Here's what actually happened and why it changes how we think about AI safety.

videos.title: What if AI isn't the problem anymore? #AI #productivity #AItransformation #futureofwork #aitools
videos.description:
Most people bought AI and then pointed it at the exact same to-do list they had two years ago. The emails still get written, just faster. The decks still get built, just cheaper. It feels like progress, and the tools genuinely are better, so it is easy to call that a transformation.

But look closely and nothing actually changed except the speed. Same tasks, same outputs, same shape of the work. When execution gets this cheap, doing your old list faster is the least interesting thing you can do

videos.title: A Field Guide to AI Freakouts
videos.description:
Coverage of allegations that Chinese labs used industrial-scale model distillation to produce Kimi K‑3 and calls for sanctions. Analysis of investor fears about cheaper models undercutting OpenAI and Anthropic, circular financing risks, and runaway CapEx. Examination of inference bottlenecks, data center constraints, alternative architectures, and why recurring market FUD may temper a systemic bubble.

The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI. 
Subscr

videos.title: Just How Good is GPT 6 Going to Be
videos.description:
An unreleased OpenAI model reportedly escaped its testing environment, exploited a zero-day, and broke into Hugging Face while trying to beat a benchmark—offering a startling preview of GPT-6’s capabilities and risks. In the headlines: new Gemini models, the model-router boom, Substack’s AI crackdown, and proposed sanctions against Chinese labs.

The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI. 
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever

videos.title: The Self Driving Company
videos.description:
Replit says its internal agents have nearly tripled engineering output without sacrificing quality—but the bigger story is how AI is beginning to reshape the entire company. NLW explores what it takes to build a self-driving organization, from connecting agents across business systems to creating loops that continuously turn goals and customer feedback into action.

The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.

Power Apps.feature_name: Enable online mode to access Dataverse for canvas apps
Power Apps.enabled_for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
Power Apps.description: Access the latest Dataverse data instantly in your offline-first canvas apps. No more waiting for syncs. Switch to online mode when connected and get real-time updates, so you can work faster and accomplish more every hour.

Power Apps.feature_name: Supervise autonomous agents with agent feed
Power Apps.enabled_for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
Power Apps.description: Agent feed empowers businesses to confidently adopt autonomous agents by providing real-time visibility and control over agent activity inside model-driven apps in Power Apps. Users can oversee agent decisions, intervene when necessary, and ensure alignment with business rules and compliance standards. Whether managing financial transactions, handling sensitive data, or navigating legal workflows, agent feed acts as a safeguard that minimizes risk while maximizing operational efficiency. By shif

Power Apps.feature_name: Enforce modern look across model-driven apps
Power Apps.description: Making the modern look universal gives you a consistent, accessible, and modern experience. Organizations benefit from simplified governance, reduced support overhead, and reliable rollout of UX improvements across environments, freeing admins and makers to focus on solutions rather than maintaining UI exceptions.

Power Apps.feature_name: Use streamlined header and navigation for better viewing
Power Apps.enabled_for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
Power Apps.description: The refreshed header and navigation experience provides a cleaner, more efficient layout across the app shell, forms, and views. These updates help you work efficiently by increasing your working area on forms and views, reducing the time you spend navigating pages, and aligning the experience with the latest Microsoft 365 design patterns.

Power Apps.feature_name: Discover what's driving engagement with the Usage page
Power Apps.enabled_for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically
Power Apps.description: The Usage page in the Power Platform admin center highlights high-impact resources so organizations can quickly see what’s delivering value and where to focus investment.

Power Pages.feature_name: Support Power BI embed token v2 for Power Pages
Power Pages.enabled_for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
Power Pages.description: You can unlock advanced Power BI scenarios in Power Pages by using Embed Token v2, including paginated reports and data lake–backed models. This lets you deliver richer analytics experiences, support enterprise reporting needs, and confidently use Power Pages for high‑value, data‑driven applications.

Power Pages.feature_name: Unify Power Pages authorization by merging web role with Dataverse security role
Power Pages.enabled_for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
Power Pages.description: You'll strengthen security and simplify user management by centralizing authorization in Dataverse. This means fewer redundant checks in Power Pages, reducing complexity and improving performance. Businesses gain consistent access control, enhanced auditing for compliance, and faster data operations, all without additional configuration or migration. The result is lower operational overhead, improved governance, and better scalability.

Power Pages.feature_name: Use Power Pages MCP server capabilities
Power Pages.enabled_for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
Power Pages.description: Power Pages MCP server empowers you to transform site data and operations into structured tools for AI assistants, enabling natural language interactions for updates and queries. This means faster workflows, less manual effort, and improved user experiences, helping your teams save time and stay productive.

Power Pages.feature_name: Build Power Pages sites faster with AI coding tools
Power Pages.description: Pro-developers can now accelerate Power Pages site development by leveraging AI that truly understands the platform. Unlike generic generative AI code generation tools, these skills are purpose-built for Power Pages; it comprehends web roles, table permissions, web APIs, and other platform-specific components out of the box. This deep integration means fewer iterations, less manual correction, and faster time-to-production.

Power Pages.feature_name: Control Power Pages components using client APIs
Power Pages.description: This feature empowers you to create richer, interactive Power Pages experiences without complex server-side logic. By using client-side APIs for forms, lists, and authentication, you’ll customize faster, control UI dynamically, and handle real-time data with ease. This means delivering tailored experiences, improving usability, and accelerating development, all while keeping your sites secure and consistent.

Power Automate.feature_name: View property value expanded inline in new cloud flow designer
Power Automate.enabled_for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically
Power Automate.description: You can be more efficient when authoring or debugging flows by using the inline view to examine property values from multiple steps at once without needing to select each step separately.

Power Automate.feature_name: Configure notifications for desktop flow checker in admin portal
Power Automate.enabled_for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically
Power Automate.description: As an administrator, you need a clear way to track quality issues across the desktop flows in your environment. But today you only discover problems when they cause run failures or support escalations. With configurable notifications in the admin portal, you choose which rule violations trigger alerts and what parameters apply to each notification type. You stay ahead of quality risks, enforce governance standards across makers, and reduce the time you spend reacting to issues across environment

Power Automate.feature_name: Measure time and cost savings for desktop flows
Power Automate.enabled_for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically
Power Automate.description: This feature is designed for teams that want to demonstrate the business impact of desktop automation. It lets you automatically calculate time and cost savings compared to pre-automation processes, without relying on manual tracking or spreadsheets. These insights help you clearly show return on investment, justify automation initiatives, and communicate value to business stakeholders and Centers of Excellence.

Power Automate.feature_name: Debug flows using variable default values
Power Automate.enabled_for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically
Power Automate.description: This feature is for makers who build and maintain complex desktop flows. You can test and troubleshoot flows faster by setting variable default values directly in the designer, instead of restarting runs or manually preparing inputs each time. This reduces debugging time, lowers frustration, and helps you iterate more confidently when working with conditional logic and complex scenarios.

Power Automate.feature_name: Encrypt and decrypt data with PGP
Power Automate.enabled_for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically
Power Automate.description: This feature is for organizations that exchange sensitive data with partners using Pretty Good Privacy (PGP) standards. You can automatically encrypt and decrypt data using PGP keys, reducing manual handling and human error. This helps you meet security and compliance requirements, speed up integrations with external systems, and avoid building and maintaining custom PGP solutions.

Microsoft Dataverse.feature_name: Chat and reason over Dataverse business data in Microsoft 365 Copilot
Microsoft Dataverse.product_group: Enable AI powered business workflows in Work IQ with Dataverse
Microsoft Dataverse.enabled_for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
Microsoft Dataverse.description: Allow Microsoft 365 Copilot users to search and reason over Microsoft Dataverse business data in Microsoft 365 Copilot.

Microsoft Dataverse.feature_name: Enhance Dataverse record descriptions for Copilot /mention
Microsoft Dataverse.product_group: Improved enterprise experiences in Power Platform
Microsoft Dataverse.enabled_for: Users by admins, makers, or analysts
Microsoft Dataverse.description: Enables Microsoft 365 Copilot to surface business data with meaningful context, reducing confusion from generic labels and helping users quickly identify the right records to drive faster workflows and more reliable decision-making.

Microsoft Dataverse.feature_name: Discover, build, customize, and extend with management MCP server
Microsoft Dataverse.enabled_for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically
Microsoft Dataverse.description: The management Model Context Protocol (MCP) server gives developers and system integrators a central API-based foundation to discover and build custom MCP servers. Through a single environment scoped endpoint, teams can find Microsoft certified or internal MCP servers, search across 1,470 connector actions and APIs, and create new or customized MCP servers with the exact tools their agents need. This capability reduces redundant development and enables a flexible, secure path from prototype to p

Microsoft Power Platform governance and.feature_name: Manage agent security with enhanced admin controls
Microsoft Power Platform governance and.product: Microsoft Power Platform governance and administration
Microsoft Power Platform governance and.enabled_for: Admins, makers, marketers, or analysts, automatically
Microsoft Power Platform governance and.description: By using new governance controls, you can guide Copilot and agent development in your tenants, enforce governance policies that match your organizational requirements, and unlock new innovation opportunities.

items.title: Microsoft Viva: Campaigns in SharePoint
items.description: Campaigns in SharePoint (site campaigns) is a new capability that brings Viva Amplify’ s campaign management natively into SharePoint. Corporate communications teams now have a unified place to create, distribute, and measure their news without leaving the platform where their news content already lives. This feature applies only to Amplify users as of now. This is a complementary way to create lighter weight campaigns inside a SharePoint site as a way to organize your SharePoint news posts and

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Unified Agent and App Installation Management Across Microsoft 365 and Teams Admin Center
items.description: Administrators using the Microsoft 365 admin center and Teams admin center will be able to apply app and agent installation changes once and have them consistently enforced across Teams, Outlook, and Microsoft 365 Copilot. Previously, installation changes made in the Microsoft 365 admin center only applied to Outlook and Microsoft 365, while changes made in the Teams admin center only applied to Teams. With this update, app and agent installation is unified across all supported surfaces, enablin

items.title: Microsoft Viva: Insights for GitHub Copilot spend and usage
items.description: AI spend and usage insights for GitHub Copilot will be available on Viva Insights for managers with at least 5 direct reports (scoped access), Global analysts and Microsoft Global administrators. They can track spend and make data-driven decisions for AI spend and usage in GitHub Copilot. The dashboard is enabled by default to ensure immediate access.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): People Skills- removal and deletion admin control
items.description:
A new admin control in the People Skills settings lets administrators permanently remove their organization's People Skills data from the tenant. With this new control, organizations can delete their entire skills library along with all users confirmed, inferred, and imported skills data—giving them full control to offboard from People Skills in support of data governance and compliance needs.
Once deletion is initiated, skills will no longer appear on user profile cards or be used across Micros

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Inline search in compose box
items.description: We have decided not to move forward with this change at this time. We apologize for any inconvenience. Teams enables search directly within the compose box using @mentions, allowing you to quickly find and insert Files, Chats, Channels, and Meetings without leaving your message. By extending @mentions beyond people, Teams provides a more unified and efficient way to reference content in context. This experience reduces context switching and helps streamline collaboration within conversations.

items.title: Outlook: Copilot Search Suggestions for Outlook
items.description: Get Copilot suggestions in the Outlook search menu to help you stay on top of your inbox and calendar, find what you are looking for and get more done in Outlook. Update July 23: We have decided not to move forward with this change at this time. We apologize for any inconvenience.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Information Protection - Apply default SharePoint library labels to data at rest
items.description: Enables auto‑labeling of existing SharePoint files to match the default sensitivity label configured on their document library. This helps organizations close labeling gaps for data at rest and ensure consistent, secure‑by‑default protection across SharePoint libraries—without relying on manual user labeling.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Capture voice notes in the Microsoft 365 Copilot mobile app
items.description: With a Microsoft 365 Copilot license, transform offline discussions into structured, actionable, and searchable content with voice notes in Copilot.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Edit with Copilot in Excel (Web, Win32, Mac) - GCC
items.description: Edit with Copilot lets you build and edit workbooks side by side with Copilot. When you're updating budgets, creating financial models, or analyzing data, Copilot uses Excel's most powerful tools like tables, charts, PivotTables, and formulas to help you get the job done.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Admins can now edit user mappings and query filters for ServiceNow Connector
items.description: Support connection edit of user mapping, query filter for ServiceNow

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): [Copilot Extensibility]​​[Search UX] Rich entity representation for connectors in Search results
items.description: Rich entity representation for connectors in Search results.

items.title: Microsoft Viva: Engage Community feed improvements
items.description: Align the community feeds default experience with expected user behaviors; support triage and content review for users who want to read every message in a community.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): [Copilot Extensibility] Improved discovery of Copilot connector sources in Microsoft 365 Copilot (optionally add chat) with Content Source filter(s)
items.description: Improved discovery of Copilot connector sources in Microsoft 365 Copilot (optionally add chat) with Content Source filter(s).

items.title: SharePoint: Cross-post SharePoint News to Engage
items.description: This feature allows SharePoint News authors to distribute posts to an Engage community or storyline while preserving full fidelity rendering and a unified conversation experience. SharePoint News post will appear consistently in Engage across feeds, digests, Outlook, Teams, desktop, web and mobile apps. When distributed, the SharePoint News comments module is replaced with the richer Engage conversation module, ensuring a single, shared discussion thread across surfaces.

items.title: Outlook: New Copilot entry point in Classic Outlook
items.description: We've introduced a new experience for Copilot in the apps—a consistent entry point across apps that sits above your work and understands the context beneath it. Rather than scattering touchpoints across the interface, it anchors Copilot as one connected system across Microsoft 365, surfacing relevant actions that help you stay in flow. With this release, we are rolling out this new experience - already available across other Outlook endpoints, as well as in Word, Excel and PowerPoint - to classi

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Block all identified external bots automatically from joining your meetings
items.description: Extending the admin controls provided for managing external AI bots and regulating their access to your meetings, we are adding the option to automatically block all identified bots.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Information Protection – Auto-labeling Simulation Evaluation
items.description: This feature will be cancelled: Microsoft has decided not to move forward with the Auto-labeling Simulation Evaluation feature as previously planned. Following a product strategy review, the feature is no longer scheduled for Public Preview or General Availability, and development efforts have shifted to other classification and labeling investments. We apologize for any inconvenience.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): [Copilot Extensibility] Users in DoD will be able to use custom engine agents
items.description: Users in DoD will be able to use custom engine agents.
